# UC-16.3.8 · Knowledge Article Usage vs. Ticket Volume

## Description

Self-service effectiveness measurement; are KB articles deflecting tickets? Correlating article views with ticket creation reveals deflection ROI and content gaps.

## Value

Self-service effectiveness measurement; are KB articles deflecting tickets? Correlating article views with ticket creation reveals deflection ROI and content gaps.

## Implementation

Ingest KB view events (ServiceNow KB, Confluence, SharePoint) and incident creation events. Normalize to daily buckets. Compute deflection ratio (views / tickets) — higher ratio suggests effective self-service. Track 7-day rolling average; alert when ratio drops >20% vs prior week. Segment by category: compare KB views for "password reset" vs ticket volume for same category. Identify high-ticket categories with low KB coverage for content creation prioritization.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom (ITSM API, KB platform analytics).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: KB article view counts, ticket creation rates.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest KB view events (ServiceNow KB, Confluence, SharePoint) and incident creation events. Normalize to daily buckets. Compute deflection ratio (views / tickets) — higher ratio suggests effective self-service. Track 7-day rolling average; alert when ratio drops >20% vs prior week. Segment by category: compare KB views for "password reset" vs ticket volume for same category. Identify high-ticket categories with low KB coverage for content creation prioritization.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm (sourcetype="kb:views" OR sourcetype="snow:incident")
| bin _time span=1d
| eval kb_views=if(sourcetype="kb:views",coalesce(views,1),0)
| eval ticket_count=if(sourcetype="snow:incident",1,0)
| stats sum(kb_views) as kb_views, sum(ticket_count) as ticket_count by _time
| eval deflection_ratio=round(kb_views/ticket_count, 2)
| streamstats window=7 avg(deflection_ratio) as avg_ratio
| eval trend=if(deflection_ratio>avg_ratio,"improving","declining")
```

Understanding this SPL

**Knowledge Article Usage vs. Ticket Volume** — Self-service effectiveness measurement; are KB articles deflecting tickets? Correlating article views with ticket creation reveals deflection ROI and content gaps.

Documented **Data sources**: KB article view counts, ticket creation rates. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Custom (ITSM API, KB platform analytics). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: kb:views, snow:incident. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="kb:views". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **kb_views** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ticket_count** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **deflection_ratio** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `streamstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **trend** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ServiceNow list or form that matches this data (for example the incident, request, or problem view). Reconcile a few record numbers, states, and times. Confirm the add-on poll or export time lines up with what you see in the browser.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (KB views vs ticket volume over time), Single value (deflection ratio), Bar chart (ratio by category), Table (top-deflecting articles).

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm (sourcetype="kb:views" OR sourcetype="snow:incident")
| bin _time span=1d
| eval kb_views=if(sourcetype="kb:views",coalesce(views,1),0)
| eval ticket_count=if(sourcetype="snow:incident",1,0)
| stats sum(kb_views) as kb_views, sum(ticket_count) as ticket_count by _time
| eval deflection_ratio=round(kb_views/ticket_count, 2)
| streamstats window=7 avg(deflection_ratio) as avg_ratio
| eval trend=if(deflection_ratio>avg_ratio,"improving","declining")
```

## Visualization

Line chart (KB views vs ticket volume over time), Single value (deflection ratio), Bar chart (ratio by category), Table (top-deflecting articles).
